analysis.reasoning: Core message: Digital technology (e.g., mobile contact-tracing apps) can help fight coronavirus and enable a gradual return to normal, but citizens must be assured of data privacy, confidentiality, and security. There is no explicit reference to the EU, its unity, success, or global actions. Evaluation by narrative: EU1 (EU successful story): Not promoted—no mention of EU history or achievements. EU2 (EU united): Not promoted—no reference to EU solidarity or agreements. EU3 (EU useful for its citizens): Not promoted—the tweet discusses digital tools and privacy in general terms without attributing policies or actions to the EU or showing EU measures improving lives within the Union. EU4 (EU avant-garde actor): Not promoted—while it mentions digital tech and privacy/security, it does not position the EU as a pioneer or regulator. EU5 (EU global champion of fair causes): Not promoted—no mention of human rights leadership by the EU. EU6 (EU contributes to a better world): Not promoted—no reference to EU’s global cooperation or contributions. Conclusion: No listed EU narrative is clearly promoted.
